Title: JA-responsive R2R3-type MYB transcription factor OsMYB4P confers broad-spectrum antiviral immunity in rice

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: polyunsaturated fatty acids, likely α-linolenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids at the 13-position to generate hydroperoxides that feed JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key JA biosynthetic gene directly activated by OsMYB4P; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant antiviral defence and jasmonate signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CM-LOX1 expression is associated with increased JA accumulation and stronger antiviral defence signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CM-LOX1 was significantly up-regulated in RSV-infected OsMYB4P-overexpressing rice and reduced in osmyb4p mutants. 2. OsMYB4P directly activated the CM-LOX1 promoter in dual-luciferase assays and bound its promoter ACCGGT motif in EMSA. 3. Increased CM-LOX1 expression in OE-OsMYB4P plants accompanied elevated JA content, linking CM-LOX1 to JA-mediated antiviral defence.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by RSV infection; induced in OsMYB4P-overexpressing plants; induced by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by RSV infection; induced by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CM-LOX1 promoter (endogenous); CM-LOX1pro in transient reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase transient transcription assay; EMSA; JA content assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for boosting endogenous JA-mediated antiviral defence, especially in combination with activating regulators such as OsMYB4P; caution that elevated JA signaling may penalize growth.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced JA signaling often inhibits growth, consistent with stronger MeJA-induced root growth inhibition in OsMYB4P OE backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CM-LOX1 is treated here as a core metabolism-relevant downstream pathway gene because it is the direct JA biosynthetic target mechanistically validated in the study, although the primary experimental focus is the regulator OsMYB4P.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-type MYB family; two SANT domains with conserved R2R3 repeats
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: mediates jasmonate signaling and antiviral defence against SRBSDV and RSV
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: jasmonic acid biosynthesis and JA-responsive defence gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; JAZ-sensitive repression; activity depends on self-oligomerization
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates CM-LOX1 transcription; positively influences JA pathway genes including CM-LOX2, OsAOS2 and OsJAZ11 under JA/virus contexts; promotes PR1a, PR2 and PR5 expression during RSV infection
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive JA-responsive regulator that directly activates a JA biosynthetic gene (CM-LOX1), raising JA levels and strengthening broad-spectrum antiviral defence
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: jasmonic acid / methyl jasmonate; viral infection; repression by OsJAZ proteins
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely participates in positive feedforward JA amplification by activating JA biosynthesis gene CM-LOX1 while itself being JA-induced; constrained by JAZ-mediated negative feedback/homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsJAZ4; OsJAZ6; OsJAZ9; OsJAZ11; SP8; P2; OsMYB4P
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms self-interacting higher-order oligomeric complex; interaction/self-oligomerization disrupted by viral proteins SP8 and P2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly induced by MeJA; functionally associated with antiviral response upon RSV and SRBSDV infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes JA accumulation/signaling and enhances resistance to SRBSDV and RSV; knockout increases susceptibility while overexpression increases resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYB4P is a JA-responsive nuclear R2R3-MYB transcriptional activator in rice. 2. It directly binds the CM-LOX1 promoter and activates CM-LOX1 expression, increasing JA content and JA sensitivity. 3. Overexpression enhances, while knockout weakens, broad-spectrum antiviral defence against SRBSDV and RSV. 4. OsJAZ proteins interact with and repress OsMYB4P. 5. Viral proteins SP8 and P2 interact with OsMYB4P, disrupt its self-oligomerization, weaken DNA binding and transactivation, and promote ubiquitin/26S proteasome-dependent destabilization.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression; virus inoculation phenotyping; qRT-PCR; Western blot of viral proteins; RNA-seq (CRA024685); dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; BiFC; Co-IP; native PAGE; JA content assay; MeJA root inhibition assay; ubiquitination assay; MG132 rescue assay
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S in transient assays and overexpression constructs; native CM-LOX1 promoter in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: RSV infection; SRBSDV infection; MeJA treatment; MG132 proteasome inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering broad-spectrum antiviral rice through overexpression, promoter tuning, or editing to reduce JAZ/viral interference while preserving agronomic fitness; may be stacked with JA-pathway genes. Monitor possible growth costs from elevated JA signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced JA signaling is associated with stronger root growth inhibition under MeJA; possible growth-defense tradeoff expected though yield penalty not directly reported.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported to regulate phosphate acquisition in rice, indicating pleiotropy beyond antiviral defence. Viral proteins SP8 and P2 synergize with OsJAZ11 to further suppress OsMYB4P activity. Protein stability is reduced via enhanced ubiquitination when viral proteins are present.
